description_long: This house features a blocky, modular design with multiple interconnected cubic volumes, giving it a contemporary and geometric appearance. The exterior is predominantly gray, with subtle variations in tone, and the structure appears to be constructed from concrete or a similar material. A prominent staircase and entry platform, rendered in a muted greenish hue, connects the main building to a lower outdoor area, suggesting a split-level or terraced layout. The roof is flat, consistent with modernist architectural styles, and the windows are set in a regular, grid-like pattern across the facade. The building is composed of two main sections, with the larger section containing the majority of the living space and the smaller section possibly serving as an annex or secondary unit. The overall impression is of a robust, urban residential building designed for durability and functionality.
description: A modern, blocky house with flat roofs, gray concrete-like exterior, and a green staircase entryway. The structure features modular cubic forms and a grid-like window arrangement.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the house from an angle highlighting the main entry staircase and the modular, blocky facade.
description_view_1: View 1 presents a head-on view of the main facade, emphasizing the symmetry and grid-like window placement.
description_view_2: View 2 displays the rear and side of the house, revealing the connection between the two main sections and the flat roof design.
description_view_3: View 3 offers a perspective from the opposite side, showcasing the green entry platform and the stepped outdoor area.
attribute_entryway_design: Main entry features a wide staircase, raised platform, and minimalistic design with no porch or decorative columns.
